data.description:
# Criteria registry + generic orchestrator/chunker

## Why
Every other workstream consumes criteria as data. Without a declarative registry and a generic chunker, criteria would be hard-coded and chunking would need hand-maintained lists that rot as criteria are added/tuned.

## What
A declarative criterion descriptor and a generic orchestrator that reads it. Reuse rebar.llm's catalog.json + contracts + prompt-runner extension points.

## Scope
- Descriptor fields: `id`, `name`, `exec` (DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP | AGENT), `facet` (concern/context it examines), `context_needs` (plan-text | plan+children | codebase | web), `routing` (base | leaf | container | overlay), `trigger` (overlay relevance signal), `checklist`, `block_policy` (advisory | blocking-with-override).
- A generic chunker: filter applicable criteria → bucket by `exec` tier → pack same-`facet` single-turn criteria up to the configured chunk size → emit one unit per AGENT criterion → DET as code. No per-criterion orchestrator code.
- Out of scope: the criteria content (sibling stories) and the chunk-size policy (chunking-routing-budget story).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A schema-validated criterion descriptor with the fields above, stored in the rebar.llm catalog.
- [ ] A generic chunker that, given a descriptor set + a chunk size, produces tier-bucketed, facet-packed chunks deterministically (unit-tested on a fixture registry).
- [ ] Adding or retuning a criterion requires only editing the registry (declaring exec+facet) — no orchestrator change; covered by a test that adds a fixture criterion and asserts it is packed correctly.
data.title: Criteria registry + generic orchestrator/chunker
